frozen_components: seed data generation LLM (stronger baseline model)
architecture.summary: R-PRM is a process reward model that evaluates intermediate reasoning steps in LLM outputs. It uses stronger LLMs to generate seed training data from limited annotations, trains a PRM via preference optimization to predict step-level correctness scores, and applies inference-time scaling to enhance evaluation. The model outputs process-level reward signals that guide or improve downstream mathematical reasoning.

claims.statement: R-PRM surpasses strong baselines by 13.9 F1 scores on ProcessBench
R-PRM surpasses strong baselines by 8.5 F1 scores on PRMBench
When applied to guide mathematical reasoning, R-PRM achieves consistent accuracy improvements of over 8.6 points across six challenging datasets
R-PRM leverages stronger LLMs to generate seed data from limited annotations, effectively activating reasoning capabilities and enabling comprehensive step-by-step evaluation
R-PRM explores self-improvement of the PRM through preference optimization, without requiring additional annotated data
R-PRM introduces inference time scaling to fully harness the model's reasoning potential
R-PRM exhibits more comprehensive evaluation and robust generalization compared to baselines
